data.title: AbbVie, Genmab announce late-stage trial setback for lymphoma therapy
data.description:
AbbVie (ABBV) and Genmab (GMAB) announced that their jointly developed bispecific antibody epcoritamab, marketed as Epkinly in the U.S., failed to meet the primary endpoint in a late-stage trial for patients with diffuse large B-cell lymphoma.

Citing topline data from their Phase 3 EPCORE DLBCL-1 study, the companies announced in January that epcoritamab didn’t lead to a statistically significant improvement in overall survival, the gold standard for measuring the efficacy of a cancer therapy.

The global trial was designed to have different primary endpoints by region, and overall survival was the sole primary endpoint in the U.S., AbbVie (ABBV) and Genmab (GMAB) clarified in a statement late Thursday.

Epcoritamab is currently available in the U.S. under the FDA's accelerated approval pathway for adults with relapsed or refractory DLBCL. The ongoing EPCORE DLBCL-1 trial is evaluating it against the investigator's choice of chemotherapy in adults with R/R DLBCL for whom high-dose chemotherapy and autologous stem cell transplant are not indicated.

data.title: Lufax announces board changes, CFO resignation
data.description:
• Lufax (LU) announced board and senior management changes effective July 25, 2026.
  • Fangfang Cai, Shibang Guo, and Peifeng Li resigned as non-executive directors and stepped down from their board committee roles.
  • Tongzhuan Xi resigned as executive director, chief financial officer, and Hong Kong authorised representative.
  • The internal team will temporarily oversee CFO responsibilities during the transition.
  • CEO Xiang Ji has been appointed as the company's Hong Kong authorised representative, replacing Tongzhuan Xi.
  • Wai Kin Chim was appointed as an independent non-executive director for an initial three-year term, effective July 25, 2026.

data.title: Mobix Labs to acquire Vision Aerial for $15M
data.description:
• Mobix Labs (MOBX) has agreed to acquire Vision Aerial, a U.S.-based designer and manufacturer of unmanned aerial systems, for approximately $15M, payable in a combination of cash and Mobix Labs common stock.
  • The transaction is expected to close this quarter, subject to customary closing conditions, including shareholder approval.
  • Upon closing, Mobix Labs expects to integrate Vision Aerial into its expanding autonomous systems business while retaining Vision Aerial’s key leadership and U.S.-based manufacturing operations.
  • The deal accelerates Mobix Labs’ global aerial intelligence strategy and National Security Matters Initiative.
  • MOBX shares rose over 8% premarket to $2.07.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: NextEra Energy Q2 adjusted EPS beats at $1.15 despite revenue miss; reaffirms long-term growth targets
Dell in focus as Citi adds 90-day catalyst watch
data.description:
• Dell Technologies (DELL) was in focus on Friday as Citi added a 90-day catalyst watch to the technology giant.
  • Shares rose fractionally in premarket trading.
  • “We are opening an upside Catalyst Watch on DELL ahead of its July-Q earnings report,” analyst Asiya Merchant wrote in a note to clients. “Our conviction has increased that enterprise IT infrastructure spending continues to strengthen, supported by ongoing AI-related investments, server refresh cycles, and broader demand for data center capacity. We see upside driven by a larger CPU total addressable market (TAM), robust server demand, and continued strength in AI infrastructure deployments, which support stronger growth.”
  • Merchant has a Buy rating and a $515 price target on Dell.

data.title: Bullish moves ahead with Equiniti carve-out
data.description:
• Bullish (BLSH) said an affiliate of Siris has exercised its previously disclosed option to acquire three non-core Equiniti business lines—EQ Retirement Solutions, EQ Customer Resolutions and Lenvi.
  • The carve-out will be completed alongside Bullish's planned $4.2B acquisition of Equiniti, which is expected to close in January 2027.
  • The company also received competition law approvals for the Equiniti acquisition from the U.K., U.S. and Germany.

data.title: Canadian National Railway Non-GAAP EPS of $2.08 beats by C$0.12, revenue of C$4.75B beats by C$130M
data.description:
• Canadian National Railway press release (CNI): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of C$2.08 beats by C$0.12.
  • Revenue of C$4.75B (+11.2% Y/Y)  beats by C$130M.
  • Raised 2026 financial guidance, now assuming low single-digit RTM growth and expecting mid-to-high single-digit adjusted diluted EPS growth
  • Operating ratio, defined as operating expenses as a percentage of revenues, of 62.5%, an increase of 80 basis points, and adjusted operating ratio of 62.2%, an increase of 50 basis points.

data.title: World Acceptance Non-GAAP EPS of $2.12 beats by $1.54, revenue of $139.21M misses by $4.69M
data.description:
• World Acceptance press release (WRLD): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.12 beats by $1.54.
  • Revenue of $139.21M (+4.8% Y/Y) misses by $4.69M.
  • Gross loans outstanding increased 2.3% to $1.29 billion at June 30, 2026, from $1.26 billion at June 30, 2025;
  • Loans 0-60 days past due on a recency basis decreased from 19.2% as of June 30, 2025 to 18.1% as of June 30, 2026; and
  • Loans 61 days or more past due on a recency basis decreased from 5.4% as of June 30, 2025 to 5.2% as of June 30, 2026.

data.title: Verizon Communications in charts: Service revenue reaches $29.23B in Q2 '26 as Wireless Equipment slumps 19.8% Y/Y
Novo files injunction to block Lilly’s weight-loss drug ads
data.description:
Novo Nordisk (NVO) on Friday said it filed a preliminary injunction to immediately block Eli Lilly (LLY) from running obesity and diabetes drug advertisements in the U.S.

The filing in a federal court in the District of New Jersey came days after the company sued its U.S. rival, alleging that Lilly's advertising campaign for GLP-1 products misled American consumers.

Novo (NVO) on Tuesday announced the lawsuit, which alleged that the Indiana-based drugmaker engaged in false advertising by claiming its GLP-1 treatments were superior.

At the time, the company said it would file a preliminary injunction against Lilly (LLY) if the Zepbound maker does not voluntarily remove the disputed ads. Lilly (LLY) stood by the advertisements, noting that it will “vigorously defend” against Novo’s (NVO) claims.

"Novo Nordisk is seeking a court order on ‌an ⁠expedited basis to ensure people can access the accurate, complete picture of the treatments available to them today,” the Danish drugmaker said.

The lawsuit filed in U.S. District Court ‌in New Jersey alleged that Lilly violated federal and state laws through its nationwide advertising campaigns for its GLP-1 drugs Mounjaro and Zepbound.

Novo (NVO) claimed that the commercials were misleading as they compared the highest doses of Lilly’s products with lower doses of its competing GLP-1s, Ozempic and Wegovy, while omitting a new higher-dose version that it said generates higher weight loss.

Lilly (LLY) didn’t immediately respond to a request for comment on Novo’s (NVO) injunction.
